name: MLKit RegionExp
summary: MLKit's IR is a region-annotated lambda calculus (Tofte-Talpin, 1994). Every value lives in a region, every region has a stack-discipline lifetime, and every allocation is letregion ρ in e. The IR carries region variables on every type and effect signatures on every function.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: MLKit's IR is a region-annotated lambda calculus (Tofte-Talpin, 1994). Every value lives in a region, every region has a stack-discipline lifetime, and every allocation is `letregion ρ in e`. The IR carries region variables on every type and effect signatures on every function: `f : (int -> int) at ρ_1 with ε`.

sections.title: Region inference on annotated IR
sections.content:
The region inference algorithm (Algorithm R, polymorphic-recursive) operates directly on this annotated IR. The IR shape is what makes inference tractable: effect annotations make it visible which regions a function may allocate in, so inference can place `letregion` boundaries optimally.

This is the canonical example of an IR designed around a specific compile-time discipline. The annotations are not auxiliary metadata; they are part of the type system the compiler enforces and the inference algorithm consumes.
